annotations.label: Pure mathematics

anchor.snippet: pure mathematics is an informal term to describe the study of mathematical concepts independently of any application outside mathematics

annotations.note: An informal meta-mathematical descriptor with no formal mathematical content to formalize in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gravitation implies conic-section orbits

anchor.snippet: his law of universal gravitation implied that planets move in orbits that are conic sections

annotations.note: Mathlib contains no orbital mechanics / Kepler problem deriving conic-section trajectories from an inverse-square force law.

annotations.label: Factoring large integers and RSA

anchor.snippet: the problem of factoring large integers , which is the basis of the RSA cryptosystem

annotations.note: Mathlib has integer factorization primitives but no formalization of the RSA cryptosystem or its hardness basis.

annotations.label: Continuous nowhere-differentiable functions

anchor.snippet: continuous functions that are nowhere differentiable

annotations.note: The Weierstrass continuous-but-nowhere-differentiable function is constructed in Counterexamples/NowhereDifferentiable.lean, which lies outside the Mathlib/ library proper.

annotations.label: Irrationality proof of the Pythagoreans

anchor.snippet: the proof irrationality which leads to the paradox that an infinite non repetitive set of digits

annotations.note: The classical Pythagorean result that √2 is irrational is stated directly as `irrational_sqrt_two`.

annotations.label: Zeno's paradox

anchor.snippet: splitting distance by two is always possible

annotations.note: Zeno's paradox itself is not formalized, but its mathematical resolution—the convergent geometric series of repeated halvings—is captured by `tsum_geometric_two`.

annotations.label: Weil conjectures

anchor.section: Finiteness, Number theory and Algebraic geometry
anchor.snippet: The success in the proof of Weil conjectures

annotations.note: Mathlib does not contain a statement or proof of the Weil conjectures (Riemann hypothesis over finite fields, etc.).

annotations.label: Four color theorem

anchor.snippet: numerical proofs of the four color theorem

annotations.note: The four color theorem is not present in Mathlib (it appears only as an open entry in docs/1000.yaml).

annotations.label: Erlangen program view of geometry

anchor.snippet: viewing geometry as the study of a space together with a group of transformations

annotations.note: Group-action infrastructure exists in Mathlib but there is no formalization of the Erlangen-program framing of geometry as a space with its transformation group.

annotations.label: Cylinder from rotation of a rectangle

anchor.snippet: deducing the form of a cylinder from the rotation of a rectangle about one of its sides

annotations.note: Mathlib has no construction of solids of revolution generating a cylinder by rotating a rectangle.
